name_en: Paved Stone Road of Kurosawa Pass
description_en: A paved stone road at Kurosawa Pass classified as a monument (historic site). It is town-designated in Oguni Town, designated on 1982-03-31, and located in Kurosawa.

description_en: The site of the Sasō magistrate's residence classified as a monument (historic site). It is town-designated in Oguni Town, designated on 1985-03-31, and located in Masuoka.

A statue of Prince Shōtoku classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of sculpture. It is town-designated in Oguni Town, designated on 1985-03-31, and located at Nikkōin in Taikozawa.

A war fan (gunsen) classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of crafts. It is town-designated in Oguni Town, designated on 1983-03-31, and located in Oguni Kosakamachi.

name_en: Baton of Command Bestowed by Lord Uesugi Kagekatsu
description_en: A commander's baton (saihai) bestowed by Lord Uesugi Kagekatsu classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of crafts. It is town-designated in Oguni Town, designated on 1984-03-31, and located in Oguni Kosakamachi.

name_en: Folding Fan Bestowed by Lord Uesugi Kagekatsu
description_en: A folding fan bestowed by Lord Uesugi Kagekatsu classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of crafts. It is town-designated in Oguni Town, designated on 1984-03-31, and located in Oguni Kosakamachi.

name_en: Painting of the Transformations of Hell
description_en: A painting depicting the transformations of hell (jigoku hensō-zu) classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of painting. It is town-designated in Oguni Town, designated on 1984-03-31, and located at Enchōji in Asasasa.

name_en: Kagetora's Regulations for the Funai Ōhashi Ferry Point
description_en: Regulations issued by Kagetora for the Funai Ōhashi ferry point, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of old documents. It is town-designated in Oguni Town, designated on 1984-03-31, and located in Oguni Kosakamachi.

name_romaji: Higashiyama Konno Iseki Shutsudo no Kokuyōseki-sei Sekki

description_en: Obsidian stone tools excavated from the Higashiyama Konno site, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of archaeological materials. It is town-designated in Oguni Town, designated on 2019-03-26, and located in Iwaisawa.
A lion dance classified as an intangible folk cultural property. It is town-designated in Oguni Town, designated on 1984-03-31, and located in Gomizawa.

Furuta kabuki theater classified as an intangible folk cultural property. It is town-designated in Oguni Town, designated on 1992-03-31, and located in Furuta.

name_en: Memorial Tower for Vengeful Spirits at Ōrizawa Pass
description_en: A memorial tower for vengeful spirits at Ōrizawa Pass classified as a tangible folk cultural property. It is town-designated in Oguni Town, designated on 1985-03-31, and located in Tamagawa.
A Buddhist nirvana image (nehanzō) at Jōsenji classified as a tangible cultural property, designated as a town cultural property. It is town-designated in Ōishida Town.
A weeping cherry tree classified as a monument, designated as a town natural monument. It is town-designated in Ōishida Town.

name_en: Gifu Butterfly and Lesser Gifu Butterfly
description_en: The Gifu butterfly and lesser Gifu butterfly, classified as a monument and designated as a town natural monument. It is town-designated in Ōishida Town.
A Chinese-style gate (karamon) of Jōfukuji classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of structures. It is municipally designated in Sakata City and designated on 1963-03-09.

name_romaji: Kyū Shirasaki Iin Tsuketari Ryō Benjo Tomomachisho
name_en: Former Shirasaki Clinic, with Two Lavatories and Waiting Room
description_en: The former Shirasaki Clinic together with two lavatories and an attendants' waiting room, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of structures. It is municipally designated in Sakata City and designated on 1978-09-08.

name_romaji: Kyū Kōkyū Bunko Honkan Tsuketari Shoko Fuzokuya Shitsunai Sōshokuhin narabini Kenchiku Kōji Kankei Shiryō
name_en: Former Kōkyū Library Main Building, with Storehouse, Annex, Interior Decorations, and Construction Records
description_en: The former Kōkyū Library main building together with its storehouse, annex, interior decorations, and construction-related materials, classified as a tangible cultural property in the category of structures. It is municipally designated in Sakata City and designated on 1996-03-01.
